115275.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

115276.explanation: We are not supporters of referenda; the low turnout shows there is little demand for them. Referenda lack the careful weighing of pros and cons that you usually have in decision-making within the municipal council.

115278.explanation: The granting of permits is not a revenue model for the municipality. Without good substantiation, this is not an issue.

115280.explanation: We do expect distribution centers to create employment. If not, we are not in favor of their establishment. Because the space on the Harlingen business parks is limited.

115281.explanation: Harlingen must also contribute its share to asylum seeker reception.

115286.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one big fireworks show organized as a community. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

115287.explanation: That must remain so. Garbage, old mattresses, and other rubbish on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is to collect bulky waste for free. This reduces hassle and results in cleaner streets for us.

115288.explanation: The noise standards must remain the same. Enforcement of the current standards is important.

115289.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. But many important places are still not well accessible, for example for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adjustments easily. Therefore, we believe the municipality can step in when needed.

115291.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. And they keep you healthy. But for people with social assistance benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can join a sports club for free. We are considering introducing a city pass.

115294.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

115296.explanation: There is already a library in the city; the villages currently do not have one.

115298.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

115300.explanation: It must be prevented that people borrow too much and get into financial trouble. More social rental homes are also needed so people can start their housing career from there.

115303.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

115305.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

115307.explanation: Currently, some people fall outside schemes because they earn just too much, while they do not have much. Wherever possible, we want to do something about that.

115309.explanation: If residents point out locations, the municipality should be receptive.

115311.explanation: However, we wonder whether there is a sufficiently diverse range to make this possible.

115318.explanation: No 'priority', but we do want to maintain the national allocation quota for rental homes for refugees with a residence status.

115320.explanation: As long as there is still space for solar panels on, for example, barn roofs, this is not necessary.

115321.explanation: The current Derde Haad no longer meets the broad desire for an outdoor pool, and a sea pool does not seem feasible. We prefer to investigate whether a partly covered, partly outdoor pool is possible. This could possibly be located at ‘t Derde Haad.

115324.explanation: With two coffeeshops, there is already enough supply in Harlingen.

115326.explanation: The current maximum building height of 20 meters allows sufficient new construction; a higher building height is not needed. Taller buildings also do not fit Harlingen.

115327.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. That is why we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented for a fair price.

115328.explanation: In our opinion, Midlum and Wijnaldum are not interested in this. Both villages already have a well-functioning community center.

115329.explanation: The parking rates have just been adjusted; we want to first await their effects.

115331.explanation: The Grote Bredeplaats is already closed off. The Voorstraat will be closed in the afternoons during the summer season, which seems sufficient to us.

115332.explanation: We want to investigate whether a partly covered outdoor pool is possible. The best location still needs to be researched.

115334.explanation: Due to national legislation concerning undermining, access to certain parts of the harbor must be prohibited. We strive to keep these restrictions limited, for example by keeping the Willemshaven accessible.

115275.explanation: It concerns the qualities and competence of the person.

115276.explanation: Leads to more involvement and trust in politics.

115278.explanation: This depends on how the municipality manages their money.

115280.explanation: This fits within the municipality's industrial policy.

115281.explanation: The final responsibility lies at the national level! This has not yet been addressed, resulting in this bottleneck being passed to the municipality. So first clarity is needed at the national level.

115286.explanation: This promotes unity within the municipality.

115287.explanation: This prevents uncontrolled littering in the street image.

115288.explanation: Hearing damage costs the municipality money.

115291.explanation: Fortunately, this already applies within the municipality of Harlingen. Think of the Sports and Culture Fund, participation fund, and for children, Sam & Leergeld.

115294.explanation: This should definitely be possible, provided there is responsible governance policy from the association.

115296.explanation: Especially in the villages, there should be a library branch. People who find it difficult to come to Harlingen also deserve the chance to borrow and read.

115298.explanation: This promotes the safety of citizens.

115300.explanation: If there were national policy for this, we would align with it. This falls under self-direction.

115303.explanation: This helps against the housing shortage.

115305.explanation: Provided this is possible for the person involved.

115307.explanation: More people are falling through the cracks due to inflation.

115309.explanation: Many parking spaces with charging facilities in the municipality are often empty.

115311.explanation: Care should not be mandatory; this is not conducive to reintegration.

115318.explanation: We advocate a level playing field, unless there is an acute safety issue for individuals.

115320.explanation: Under the current situation, we cannot connect the panels; furthermore, there is no solution yet on how to recycle them.

115321.explanation: Consideration should also be given to the option of creating an outdoor pool.

115324.explanation: When this is prohibited, it encourages more illegal trade.

115326.explanation: This is vandalism of the streetscape of our beautiful historic city.

115327.explanation: Unless this falls under recreation and short-term rental—think of AirBnB or BnB.

115328.explanation: This is precisely a nice place to organize events so that it is accessible for everyone in the municipality, and there could also be room for a library here. This center fosters unity within the village.

115329.explanation: Cars are already being banned to the outer rings of the city at this moment.

115331.explanation: This obstructs accessibility to the entrepreneurs.

115332.explanation: Because expanding the third swimming pool would make this no longer necessary.

115334.explanation: In relation to the safety of the residents.

115275.explanation: We prefer party aldermen who have a connection with the municipality. The gender ratio is not leading, but based on qualities.

115276.explanation: Only for major issues. For example, the independence of the municipality or expansion of municipal boundaries.

115278.explanation: Given the general cost increases, the CDA does not want the costs of municipal services to rise and wants to reduce them where possible.

115280.explanation: CDA wants to create space for entrepreneurs with a view to more employment.

115281.explanation: We must accommodate people from war zones out of humanity.

115286.explanation: The municipality already organizes a fireworks show during the Fisheries Days.

115288.explanation: Standards have already been set for this.

115289.explanation: The CDA supports initiatives that map out the accessibility of shops. The CDA encourages entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible, often by promoting quality marks such as Drempelvrij and the Ongehinderd app. The CDA does not see this as a task for the municipality.

115294.explanation: Financial support. For example, with a loan.

115296.explanation: With additional provisions for the villages.

115298.explanation: Only in the harbor due to criminal activities and temporarily allowed when there are emergencies, for example if there is an active arsonist.

115300.explanation: A starter loan gives young people perspective on the housing market. With a starter loan, we ensure that starters actually get a fair chance to buy a home in our municipality.

115303.explanation: The CDA no longer wants to allow the sale of homes in the city center for recreational purposes. This harms the livability.

115305.explanation: For the CDA, doing nothing is not an option: you work, go to school or do volunteer work unless the aforementioned is not possible due to physical or psychological reasons. It cannot be that someone lives at society’s expense without any form of return in any capacity.

115307.explanation: The municipality of Harlingen currently applies an income test of 130%. That is acceptable to us.

115309.explanation: Enforce against charging freeloaders. (Cars left parked after being charged)

115311.explanation: Discriminating against a caregiver in this way is against Article 1 of the Constitution.

115318.explanation: The municipality must retain the freedom to prioritize vulnerable groups according to its own insight. Not only status holders.

115321.explanation: The CDA stands for maintaining facilities in our municipality. This also applies to the Derde Haad swimming pool. A future-proof, sustainable pool must be considered.

115324.explanation: We do not want to allow a second coffeeshop.

115326.explanation: A maximum of 6/7 stories can be built if fitting within the cityscape.

115327.explanation: The house must be suitable for room rental according to the zoning plan, which the municipality can already act against.

115328.explanation: A strong community life is important from a multifunctional center or village hall. This offers opportunities for clustering social functions. It is an investment in social cohesion, vital associations with their own identity.

115329.explanation: Discourage day parking in the city center. Encourage park-and-ride.

115331.explanation: Car-restricted during the tourist season from April through September.

115332.explanation: The CDA advocates for an outdoor swimming facility on the Koemen site with a beach and possibilities for organizing small cultural events.

115334.explanation: In the harbor and industrial areas, the CDA wants to ensure stricter safety checks and clear agreements with companies. Economy and safety go hand in hand.

115275.explanation: It is about substantive quality. This does not change the fact that more women in local government is desirable.

115276.explanation: Harlingen already has the possibility to hold a consultative referendum.

115278.explanation: Cost coverage and market conformity are the norm; increasing rates is not a goal in itself. So 'must'? No, it totally depends.

115280.explanation: Harlingen does not aim for large-scale distribution centers in terms of new business activity.

115281.explanation: In Harlingen there is a small-scale shelter on a ship, which suits in size and location. National measures are urgently needed.

115286.explanation: The VVD is not opposed to one fireworks show, but then in cooperation with entrepreneurs and residents, including financially. So no 'must'.

115287.explanation: Not standard to homes. It can be considered to reintroduce this periodically at central collection points.

115288.explanation: Are strict enough; events should not be made even more difficult.

115289.explanation: The municipality is responsible for public spaces, entrepreneurs for their own buildings.

115291.explanation: There are already other facilities for financially vulnerable people to be able to participate in sports.

115294.explanation: The municipality is in talks with sports clubs to make them future-proof. Organizational and physical consolidation is desired.

115296.explanation: It is already fine as it is now.

115298.explanation: If privacy can be guaranteed. And there must be an assessment of whether and where this might be desirable and useful.

115300.explanation: It will be examined whether and how this can be effectively deployed. The VVD sees starter loans as a useful tool but mainly wants a combination of more generous borrowing options. Increase the maximum loan amount to the NHG standard so that more starters qualify.

115303.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreational use. Housing problems must be solved differently, at least in Harlingen.

115305.explanation: If regular paid work is not yet possible, then it must be examined what is possible instead.

115307.explanation: This is already higher in Harlingen than in many other municipalities.

115309.explanation: The demand will (ultimately) continue to increase, so a good plan to counteract cluttering is indeed a precondition.

115311.explanation: Affordability and availability of staff will come under further pressure in the coming years.

115318.explanation: See question 27. This should no longer be necessary in the long term. But fundamental measures are needed at the national level, and central government funding must first come to build modest and flexible housing for status holders. If this does not happen, reception problems will arise in the asylum centers.

115320.explanation: Especially in this area, the agricultural land is very fertile; therefore, in our view, solar panels do not belong on agricultural land or industrial sites.

115321.explanation: This issue will not be relevant in the coming years. But it will be in 4-5 years.

115326.explanation: What is considered high? It depends where: close to the center you really have to be careful, further away it is possibly feasible depending on nature and scale. But really tall and large apartment blocks will not be built here.

115327.explanation: Regulation is needed to prevent labor migrants from being housed everywhere.

115329.explanation: It is not a goal in itself. Parking fees in the center should be used to keep the center accessible for visitors. So: short-term parking affordable near the center, long-term parking more on the outskirts. Eilanderblik complicates this somewhat in Harlingen.

115331.explanation: That we are currently working to make Voorstraat somewhat less car-friendly and Grote Bredeplaats car-free does not mean the entire center must be car-free. The shopping area must remain easily accessible; making the entire center car-free does not help with that.

115332.explanation: The nature and scale must fit within future developments there. The VVD is more thinking of a natural recreational lake.

115334.explanation: Accessibility of the harbor entrance and the depth of the channels will remain priorities.

115275.explanation: It is about your qualities for the position, not whether you were born with a penis or a vagina. Moreover, it is impractical: we already have a female mayor, so there would only be room for a female alderman. And which party should supply her?

115276.explanation: This seems to us a good informational addition for the Council.

115278.explanation: If it is necessary, it is necessary, but nothing is “mandatory.”

115280.explanation: Harlingen does not have a large territory and must therefore strive for effective and efficient use of (business) areas. The added value in terms of employment and payroll must be taken into account, and in our opinion, a distribution center does not offer much of that.

115281.explanation: Asylum reception is part of a completely failed European and Dutch asylum system. This system must stop. Meanwhile, the consequences should not be placed on the municipalities.

115286.explanation: But that can also be viewed separately from a ban on ornamental fireworks.

115287.explanation: Nothing is free; sooner or later it costs money. With “free,” municipal taxes will go up, also for people who generate hardly any bulky waste.

115288.explanation: That will have to depend heavily on the frequency and circumstances. What applies in the center is different from in the middle of the polder.

115289.explanation: If the municipality imposes obligations, where restraint is appropriate, there should be financial compensation. Also: a reasonable cost-benefit analysis must be conducted.

115291.explanation: Questionable given the poverty trap. In any case, the money should be earmarked and perhaps it is better to subsidize the clubs more directly.

115294.explanation: First, it must be examined what causes those difficulties and what improvements are possible with a financial contribution. We will not simply open the wallet for poor policy.

115298.explanation: We must not descend into Chinese-style conditions (surveillance society), but it could help for more safety and against certain violations. Based on careful research and considerations.

115300.explanation: Given the housing shortage and the high prices, this seems very desirable to us.

115303.explanation: “Must” is a strong word, but given the housing shortage, regulated flexibility in this could be appropriate.

115305.explanation: In principle, yes. Welfare has never been intended as a kind of basic income but as a (temporary) safety net. But it should not compete with normal paid work. Socially meaningful work can help people on their way to a regular job. Customization is desirable, but beware of excessive regulation.

115307.explanation: In principle, yes. But what form will that “help” take and where does it end?

115309.explanation: Electrification as part of the so-called energy transition is failing. Moreover, electric cars are not clean at all. So pause for reflection, unless it is a demonstrably profitable investment.

115311.explanation: The description is far too broad and unclear. This threatens to further increase healthcare costs without improving care. A care recipient who has too many vague (private) expenses must pay those themselves. It is not reasonable to demand that neighbors pay for it through their premiums or taxes.

115318.explanation: We find the opposite more logical. Our government is primarily responsible for its own residents, not for half the world that wants to come here for whatever reason. Moreover, the Netherlands grants residence permits ten times more easily than other countries, which raises doubts about the necessity.

115320.explanation: Electrification is failing and agricultural land is too valuable to sacrifice to blue-black deserts.

115321.explanation: Maybe, maybe not, but first we would like a thorough justification.

115326.explanation: Around the center, we have been staunchly against this for years. It obstructs the view of the historic city center and the cityscape with ship masts.

115327.explanation: That is comparing apples and oranges. Two different markets. If there is a need for rooms or studios, they must be offered just as well as houses.

115329.explanation: Yes, but not as a goal in itself. Parking and permit fees must serve a purpose, and that purpose must guide the level of the fees. Therefore, these can differ per city district or even per street.

115331.explanation: Car-free zones or any other goal in this area should not be an end in itself, but part of a vision on the use of (parts of) the center, for example the attractiveness and viability of the city center as a shopping area, but also as a residential area.

115332.explanation: Sounds nice, but the result may be that the region ends up with two such swimming pools, both of which are not profitable (Witmarsum and Harlingen). We have a better plan in our program: a sea pool that is unique in Friesland and the wider area.

115334.explanation: There are several aspects to this topic and it is unclear what exactly is meant here.

115275.explanation: HOOP strives for quality and involvement. Although a balance is desirable, the direct connection with the Harlingen community and the expertise of candidates are paramount. We select based on passion for the city and villages, regardless of gender.

115276.explanation: We advocate maximum participation of the people of Harlingen. For major projects that affect the unique identity or historic character of our city and villages, residents must be able to express their opinion directly via a referendum.

115278.explanation: We want to keep the burden low for our residents and local entrepreneurs. Increasing fees does not align with our goal to stimulate a vital local economy and accessible services.

115280.explanation: We are very critical of large-scale developments that disrupt the historic appearance of Harlingen and the surrounding villages.

115281.explanation: HOOP chooses a human scale that does not exceed the capacity of our neighborhoods and social services. Safety, livability, and maintaining social bonds in the neighborhood are guiding principles for us.

115286.explanation: A central event can enhance safety and connect residents. However, this must not lead to cuts in essential neighborhood facilities or local care.

115287.explanation: Free bulky waste collection promotes a vital and clean living environment. This is especially an important service for seniors who do not have transportation to the recycling center.

115288.explanation: A good balance between a lively city and livability for residents is essential. We must protect Harlingen’s character without completely blocking recreational plans.

115289.explanation: Accessibility of the city is a priority. Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for making and keeping their buildings accessible.

115291.explanation: Everyone must be able to stay vital. For people with low incomes we offer a free Sports and Culture pass. This encourages exercise and social participation without finances being a barrier.

115294.explanation: Sports clubs are the driving force behind the vitality and social cohesion of our residents. When they are struggling, the municipality must step in to save these crucial facilities.

115296.explanation: There is a central library. It is located such that people from the village of Wijnaldum take almost as long to get there as people from the outskirts of Oosterpark.

115298.explanation: For safety in neighborhoods we primarily rely on more visible police presence ('blue on the street'). Cameras may be a useful addition at specific hotspots, but human supervision and enforcement are our strong preference.

115300.explanation: To keep Harlingen vital, young people must be able to build a future here. A starter loan helps them acquire a home, which is essential for the generational mix and social fabric in neighborhoods.

115303.explanation: In the municipality of Harlingen there is no holiday park with vacation homes eligible for this.

115305.explanation: Social work promotes connectedness. We especially encourage this kind of participation via our neighborhood and village centers, which we continue to financially support as the indispensable social hub of our community.

115307.explanation: We want to raise the income limit to 130% of the minimum income. This gives more households access to the Sports and Culture pass and support from the Energy Coach for an affordable energy bill.

115309.explanation: Although important for future accessibility, placement must not come at the expense of general parking options for less mobile seniors and residents. All neighborhoods and villages have a shortage of parking spaces; previously families had one car per household, now often more.

115311.explanation: Freedom of choice is not absolute; due to a tight labor market this is rarely possible. We focus on quality care and informal care in the local neighborhood that seamlessly matches the personal needs of the resident.

115318.explanation: Status holders and other refugees have never been able to register in contrast to own residents. It is basically only a few percent of the available homes. Moreover, there is national legislation regulating these matters, such as the distribution law.

115320.explanation: We prefer solar panels on roofs and parking lots. Valuable agricultural land must be preserved for our local craftsmen and the unique character of our landscape.

115321.explanation: Swimming is essential for the vitality of young and old. Maintaining good swimming facilities is a basic provision we must cherish and renew for the Harlingen community.

115324.explanation: Safety and the protection of our youth come first. We are very cautious about the establishment of coffee shops if this harms livability and the feeling of safety in our neighborhoods.

115326.explanation: High residential buildings must fit into Harlingen’s skyline. Some taller buildings already exist. The municipality has limited land, so sometimes a somewhat taller building is necessary to solve the housing shortage.

115327.explanation: We want to protect the character of our neighborhoods and prevent nuisance caused by too many residents at one address. Homes must primarily remain available for families and seniors.

115328.explanation: Preservation and financial support of village, neighborhood, and community centers is a non-negotiable condition for HOOP. They are the indispensable social meeting places that prevent loneliness and connect the community.

115329.explanation: Affordable parking in the center is necessary for accessibility of shops and facilities, especially for seniors and residents from the villages who are less mobile. It will be more expensive for long-term parkers to promote turnover for people who just need to run an errand.

115331.explanation: Accessibility of the city center must be guaranteed for less mobile residents and people who depend on private transport or transport services such as the 'automaatje'.

115332.explanation: An important priority for HOOP. The realization of an outdoor pool near the seawall stimulates sustainable day recreation and directly contributes to a vital lifestyle for all residents of the municipality.

115334.explanation: Nowadays, it is difficult to keep a port accessible to everyone. Due to increasing crime and the safety of the port area, it is completely or partially closed off, and more cameras and security measures are being installed.

115276.explanation: Focus is on citizens' assemblies as a form of substantive participation.

115278.explanation: The assessment and speed of granting permits must be prioritized. More decision-making authority should be given to a responsible (senior) official. Higher prices combined with a strong improvement in speed. The economic interest is evident.

115280.explanation: Focus is on a sustainable, circular, and local economy. The spatial impact of large-scale distribution is significant.

115281.explanation: Reception of asylum seekers is a shared responsibility. Humane reception fits with solidarity and legal state principles.

115286.explanation: We do not think fireworks are a good idea and would rather choose an alternative for which the money could be spent. A laser show with music or something similar.

115287.explanation: Waste policy is aimed at sustainable waste management.

115288.explanation: Balance between livability and cultural offerings is fundamental.

115291.explanation: Sport contributes to health and participation. Based on disposable income, not on benefits.

115294.explanation: The basis must be personal responsibility.

115296.explanation: Accessible facilities in neighborhoods and villages are important. The library also fulfills a social and educational function close to residents.

115298.explanation: Safety must be balanced against privacy. Camera surveillance can only be used specifically and under conditions.

115300.explanation: Starter loans increase purchase prices; promoting housing turnover through mid-range rent, house splitting, and inheritance division.

115303.explanation: Permanent residency requires suitable housing quality; holiday homes are not intended for structural housing.

115305.explanation: Participation is important but must be appropriate and voluntary. Stimulating is important rather than mandatory.

115307.explanation: Poverty also affects low-income workers. Early support prevents bigger problems. Disposable income must be leading.

115309.explanation: Electric driving supports the energy transition. Charging infrastructure is a prerequisite for sustainability.

115311.explanation: Quality and continuity of care must be leading.

115318.explanation: Integration and equal opportunities remain the starting point. Legal obligations require timely housing.

115320.explanation: Assessment per location is necessary, no generic ban.

115321.explanation: Basic facilities must be future-proof. Sport and exercise are important for health.

115324.explanation: Open and controlled policy fits a realistic approach. Regulation is preferable to prohibition.

115326.explanation: High-rise buildings can contribute to housing supply but require careful integration. Livability and spatial quality are preconditions.

115327.explanation: Flexible housing forms help with housing shortages. Regulation is more appropriate than a total ban.

115328.explanation: Meeting and social cohesion are important. Facilities nearby strengthen livability.

115329.explanation: Accessibility and land use must be considered together. Orientation towards discouraging car use in the center.

115331.explanation: Accessibility and land use must be considered together. Livability and accessibility must be considered alongside entrepreneurship by shopkeepers.

115332.explanation: A recreational lake near Harlingen can also fulfill this function.

115334.explanation: The port has social and economic value. Accessibility fits a broad public function including attracting youth.

115275.explanation: When forming the council, the expertise of the candidate should come first. Equal distribution between men and women is desirable but a secondary criterion.

115276.explanation: The Christian Union supports citizens' assemblies. A representative group of residents think along about complex issues. This increases support.

115278.explanation: We currently see no reason for an increase. But if municipal costs rise, it may be necessary to adjust rates; we do not rule that out.

115280.explanation: The establishment of a distribution center falls under the economic freedom of entrepreneurs. The municipality can impose additional requirements on sustainable buildings in a green environment when adjusting zoning plans or environmental permits. The Christian Union prefers no large block buildings in our landscape.

115281.explanation: Harlingen, like all municipalities in the Netherlands, is bound by the dispersion law. Additionally, the municipality must be in solidarity with other Dutch municipalities and absorb its fair share. After all, these are also the workers of the future.

115286.explanation: We would like to make a decision based on multiple alternatives, such as a drone show. We are also open to other alternatives. We would like to see the municipality take this up together with, for example, the entrepreneurs' association.

115287.explanation: The current arrangement of 6 times per year free of charge suffices.

115288.explanation: The Christian Union sees no reason for change here. However, we do want to prevent noise levels from rising further. As it is now, it can remain. The conditions in the permits must be well monitored.

115289.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs must ensure that their premises are suitable.

115294.explanation: The municipality must pay additional attention to small associations and not only consider the size of the association.

115296.explanation: The current system works well. We see no reason to change it.

115298.explanation: Camera surveillance constitutes a substantial invasion of citizens' privacy. Moreover, the footage must also be managed. Therefore, the Christian Union wants to be cautious about installing cameras. However, we are open to placing cameras at specific locations where people feel unsafe or for the protection of special groups.

115300.explanation: The municipality may impose additional requirements regarding who qualifies. Eligible are mainly young people who cannot afford it themselves.

115303.explanation: Holiday homes can play a role in reducing the housing shortage in this way. However, holiday homes must be adapted to use energy more efficiently, as they are not designed for permanent residence.

115305.explanation: We propose that the municipality invite these people to do socially meaningful work, paid or unpaid. The municipality may also provide a reward for volunteer work by this group.

115309.explanation: We consider sustainability important. Electric cars have a role to play in this, and we therefore welcome them in Harlingen.

115311.explanation: People have a free choice. The reimbursement depends on the health insurer and the Christian Union will not intervene in this.

115318.explanation: There are multiple target groups with an interest in quickly obtaining a home. The Christian Union explicitly wants to pay attention to young people starting in the labor market.

115320.explanation: We believe it is people's freedom to use their land for solar panels. However, we want to explore to what extent we can impose additional requirements for green integration and, for example, multifunctional use of the land. The municipality should set an example with the parking lots it owns.

115321.explanation: A well-considered decision must still be made after all the facts and figures are known. Alternatives include an outdoor swimming pool or connecting to the renovation of the Bloemketerp pool.

115324.explanation: The municipality is committed to a smoke-free generation. The Christian Union wholeheartedly supports this effort. Drugs and smoking cause much harm and cost society a lot.

115326.explanation: Recently, several apartment buildings have been completed. They serve as a good example. We do not find larger or taller buildings necessary as they detract from our beautiful municipality.

115327.explanation: Harlingen has (almost) no student housing (excluding the Maritime Academy in this context). Moreover, in the past, this has caused much nuisance in the center. Therefore, the Christian Union sees no added value in subdividing homes into rooms in our municipality.

115328.explanation: These already exist and must be maintained.

115329.explanation: Particularly the rates for daytime parking. Permits for residents remain unchanged. Encourage parking facilities on the city outskirts and better connect them with the center.

115331.explanation: For the Voorstraat, we support a longer closing time, giving cyclists and pedestrians ample space, with attention to parking for the disabled.

115332.explanation: This is a good combination of exercise and recreation for both young and old.

115334.explanation: To remain economically relevant, the port must comply with the so-called ISPS regulations. This means that visitors may only come to designated areas.

statements.title: A permanent outdoor swimming pool must be built near the Westerzeedijk area of Harlingen.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The residents of Harlingen must be able to give their opinion on important topics via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Harlingen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Harlingen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Harlingen must renew the 't Derde Haad swimming pool.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
There must be one larger library in Harlingen instead of smaller libraries in every village.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The center of Harlingen must be car-free.
The prices of municipal permits must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Paid parking in the city center should become more expensive.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Harlingen.
The port of Harlingen must remain accessible to everyone.
